1	# Clean-room reimplementation protocol — tamarin-rs relicensing
2	
3	Purpose: independently recreate three modules of tamarin-rs (wellformedness
4	checker, graph/dot pipeline, web layer) without access to GPL-covered
5	expression, so the results are original works relicensable at will.
6	
7	## Rooms
8	- DIRTY ROOM (Kevin Milner + main-session assistant): has read the GPL Haskell
9	  source and the ported Rust modules. Produces only: interoperability headers
10	  (type surfaces, entry-point signatures, comments stripped), oracle wrappers,
11	  and behavioral bug reports. Never contributes implementation text.
12	- CLEAN ROOM (isolated agents, one per cluster): receive ONLY the materials
13	  listed per cluster below. Their complete tool-call transcripts are retained
14	  as evidence of non-access.
15	
16	## Clean-room materials (exhaustive)
17	1. This protocol, the cluster SPEC.md, and interface/ headers (type surfaces +
18	   boundary signatures only).
19	2. Black-box oracles: `wf_oracle.sh` (runs the compiled Haskell binary on an
20	   input file), `hs_server.sh` (serves a theory in the Haskell web UI for HTTP
21	   probing), pre-captured oracle outputs (oracle/captures/,
22	   oracle/captured_responses/), and GPL example .spthy files used solely as
23	   observation INPUTS.
24	3. Public Rust toolchain and general documentation.
25	
26	## Forbidden to the clean room
27	- Reading ANY file under /home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/ — source, comments, git
28	  history — with the sole exceptions of executing the oracle scripts and
29	  reading oracle/captured_responses/ (captured program OUTPUT, not source).
30	- Fetching tamarin-prover source, docs describing its internals, or its GitHub.
31	- Reproducing any tamarin-prover implementation detail from model memory:
32	  every behavior must be derived from the oracle, the captures, or the SPEC.
33	  Exact output strings (warning texts, DOT syntax, HTML) MUST be taken from
34	  observed oracle output — that is compatibility content, not expression.
35	
36	## Audit & integration (after implementation)
37	1. Transcript audit: verify from the retained agent transcripts that no
38	   forbidden path was accessed.
39	2. Similarity audit: a reviewer (may see both sides) compares the clean code
40	   against the Haskell originals; flagged similarities are sent back as
41	   redo-instructions, never patched directly.
42	3. Behavioral acceptance: the FULL-CORPUS gate for the slice must pass, not a
43	   fixture subset (see "Full-corpus gates" below). A narrow fixture suite that
44	   passes while the corpus gate fails is not acceptance.
45	4. Integration: the open side may write thin adapters and mechanical renames to
46	   fit the workspace API, but must not transplant logic from the replaced
47	   modules. The replaced modules are deleted; their GPL-pending headers go
48	   with them (history retains them under GPL). Every integrator that touches a
49	   slice with a full-corpus gate MUST run that gate before declaring green.
50	
51	## Full-corpus gates (standard, not optional)
52	
53	Each slice has a fast oracle gate over the whole 419-file example corpus,
54	diffing the slice's output against the Haskell reference cache. These are the
55	sealed implementer's primary data source (run between build and the HS cache
56	every iteration) AND the integrator's mandatory acceptance check. Both take
57	`RS_PATH` (point at any build) and `ALLOWLIST` (subset while iterating).
58	
59	- Wellformedness: `scripts/wf_gate.sh` — runs the binary WITHOUT `--prove`
60	  (the wf block prints at theory-load, ~1s/file; full corpus ~72s at JOBS=6),
61	  extracts the `/* WARNING … */` block, diffs vs `scripts/.hs_file_cache`.
62	- Web: `scripts/web_parity.sh` — crawls HS and RS interactive servers per
63	  theory, semantic-diffs the response manifests vs `scripts/.web_hs_cache`.
64	- Full batch (proof output, slow ~30-60min): `scripts/corpus_file_diff.sh` —
65	  the ground-truth `--prove` byte diff; run at milestones, not every iteration.
66	
67	Once a slice's ported code is DELETED, the integrated module IS the sealed
68	reimplementation, so running the gate against the tree observes the sealed
69	code's own output vs the reference — legitimate feedback, not observation of
70	replaced code. For a not-yet-integrated slice the tree still holds the ported
71	code, so gate only the workspace build (via `RS_PATH`), never the stock tree.
72

1	//! producers-clean: a clean-room reimplementation of the tamarin-prover
2	//! interactive web UI's fragment PRODUCERS — the code that renders pre-computed
3	//! prover values into the HTML/JSON response-body CONTENT that the already
4	//! clean-roomed dispatch layer serves. Derived purely from black-box
5	//! observation of captured responses + the live oracle. See ../../SPEC.md for
6	//! the observable boundary and the R1–R5 sub-target decomposition, and
7	//! workspace/BEHAVIOR.md for the behavioral spec you build up as you probe.
8	//!
9	//! Status: R1 (center section fragments + the shared HTML skin), R5 (the
10	//! theory-path grammar) and R2 (the proof-script west pane assembly)
11	//! IMPLEMENTED — `html` (escape / postprocess / envelopes), `section`
12	//! (`render_pane`, `render_help_pane`), `path` (`parse` / `render`), and
13	//! `proofscript` (`render_index`, consuming R5 for every link). Gated by
14	//! tests/round1_center_fragments.rs + tests/corpus_sweep.rs (R1: 324-fragment
15	//! byte parity), tests/r5_path_grammar.rs (R5: live-probe acceptance replay +
16	//! 40037-tail corpus byte round-trip), and tests/r2_west_pane.rs (R2: all 478
17	//! overview west panes sliced and re-rendered byte-identically + live-probe
18	//! replays). R3 (structured proof-tree rendering; R2 currently takes the
19	//! proof display as pre-rendered lines) and R4 remain UNIMPLEMENTED stubs.
20	//!
21	//! In scope: pure-render producers — given pre-computed prover values, emit the
22	//! fragment bytes (tags, links, headings, escaping, line breaks, envelope).
23	//! OUT of scope (opaque input / other clusters): the pretty-printed content
24	//! text itself (formula / rule / signature / method text), the constraint
25	//! system and applicable-proof-methods panes, graph DOT/SVG, and the Rust-only
26	//! progressive-UI route.

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Black-box oracle for the WEB PRODUCER SURFACE clean-room.
3	#
4	# Serves ONE .spthy in the sanctioned Haskell interactive web UI so the sealed
5	# room can curl any route and observe the exact HTTP response body it must
6	# reproduce. Live probing complements the captured corpus (see
7	# ../round1/ + oracle/extract_fragments.py); use it to pin fragment structure,
8	# link targets and section headers a single capture leaves ambiguous.
9	#
10	# Usage:
11	#   hs_server.sh start <file.spthy> [port]   # boot + block until ready; prints URL
12	#   hs_server.sh probe <port> <path>         # curl one route, print the body
13	#   hs_server.sh stop  <port>                # kill the server + free the port
14	#   hs_server.sh smoke <file.spthy> [port]   # start, probe /overview + /main, stop
15	#
16	# Ports 3100-3199 belong to this cluster (default 3100). Everything below is
17	# derived from black-box behavior; no tamarin-rs source is read — the only
18	# tamarin-rs touch is EXECUTING this sanctioned binary.
19	#
20	# Why this wrapper exists (do not "simplify" back): the stock weblayer
21	# hs_server.sh passes `--port <n>` with a SPACE, which the prover's CmdArgs
22	# parser reads as the positional WORKDIR (`directory '<n>' does not exist`) and
23	# the server dies silently after a fixed `sleep 4`. This wrapper (a) uses
24	# `--port=<n>` (equals, like scripts/web_parity.sh), (b) POLLS for readiness
25	# instead of sleeping (maude boot + elaboration routinely take >10s), and
26	# (c) frees the port on stop (pid + process-group + listener fallback).
27	
28	set -u
29	
30	# ---- OOM guards (preserve; a runaway prover must be the kill target) --------
31	echo 1000 > /proc/self/oom_score_adj 2>/dev/null || true
32	ulimit -v 25165824 2>/dev/null || true            # 24 GB address-space cap
33	
34	export PATH="/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin:$PATH" # maude / dot live here
35	HS="${HS_PATH:-/home/kamilner/tamarin-rs/tamarin-prover-testing/.stack-work/install/x86_64-linux-tinfo6/ec0cb11b1bfcf8776d45e0357bbc6d6ff2077f9222735af22115429c8cdfcef1/9.6.7/bin/tamarin-prover}"
36	READY_TIMEOUT="${READY_TIMEOUT:-90}"
37	DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT="${DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT:-30}"    # match web_parity.sh (5s default expires on ~12 files)
38	
39	pidfile() { echo "/tmp/producers_hs_$1.pid"; }
40	wdfile()  { echo "/tmp/producers_hs_$1.wd"; }
41	logfile() { echo "/tmp/producers_hs_$1.log"; }
42	
43	free_port() {
44	  local port="$1" pid
45	  pid=$(cat "$(pidfile "$port")" 2>/dev/null || true)
46	  if [ -n "$pid" ]; then
47	    kill -TERM "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -TERM "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
48	    sleep 1
49	    kill -KILL "-$pid" 2>/dev/null || kill -KILL "$pid" 2>/dev/null || true
50	  fi
51	  # Fallback: anything still bound to the port (reaps orphaned maude children).
52	  if command -v fuser >/dev/null 2>&1; then
53	    fuser -k "${port}/tcp" 2>/dev/null || true
54	  fi
55	  local wd; wd=$(cat "$(wdfile "$port")" 2>/dev/null || true)
56	  [ -n "$wd" ] && [ -d "$wd" ] && rm -rf "$wd"
57	  rm -f "$(pidfile "$port")" "$(wdfile "$port")"
58	}
59	
60	start() {
61	  local f="$1" port="${2:-3100}"
62	  [ -f "$f" ] || { echo "no such file: $f" >&2; return 2; }
63	  [ -x "$HS" ] || { echo "no HS binary at $HS" >&2; return 2; }
64	  free_port "$port"                                # clean any stale bind first
65	  local wd; wd=$(mktemp -d); mkdir -p "$wd/thy"; cp "$f" "$wd/thy/"
66	  echo "$wd" > "$(wdfile "$port")"
67	  # setsid → own process group so `free_port` can signal the whole tree.
68	  # NOTE: `--port=<n>` (equals) is load-bearing; a space breaks arg parsing.
69	  setsid "$HS" interactive "$wd/thy" --port="$port" \
70	      --derivcheck-timeout="$DERIVCHECK_TIMEOUT" >"$(logfile "$port")" 2>&1 &
71	  echo $! > "$(pidfile "$port")"
72	  local pid; pid=$(cat "$(pidfile "$port")")
73	  local i
74	  for ((i=0; i<READY_TIMEOUT; i++)); do
75	    if curl -sf -o /dev/null "http://127.0.0.1:$port/"; then
76	      echo "ready: http://127.0.0.1:$port/  (pid $pid, ~${i}s)"
77	      return 0
78	    fi
79	    kill -0 "$pid" 2>/dev/null || { echo "server died; log:" >&2; tail -5 "$(logfile "$port")" >&2; free_port "$port"; return 2; }
80	    sleep 1
81	  done
82	  echo "not ready after ${READY_TIMEOUT}s; log:" >&2; tail -5 "$(logfile "$port")" >&2
83	  free_port "$port"; return 2
84	}
85	
86	case "${1:-}" in
87	  start) shift; start "$@";;
88	  stop)  free_port "${2:?port}"; echo "stopped $2";;
89	  probe) curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:${2:?port}${3:?path}";;
90	  smoke)
91	    f="${2:?file.spthy}"; port="${3:-3100}"
92	    start "$f" "$port" || exit 2
93	    echo "--- /thy/trace/1/overview/help (title) ---"
94	    curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:$port/thy/trace/1/overview/help" | grep -oE '<title>[^<]*</title>' | head -1
95	    echo "--- /thy/trace/1/main/message (first 200 bytes) ---"
96	    curl -s "http://127.0.0.1:$port/thy/trace/1/main/message" | head -c 200; echo
97	    free_port "$port"; echo "stopped $port (port freed)";;
98	  *) sed -n '2,33p' "$0"; exit 1;;
99	esac

## 14. Full route surface — top-level + reload/download/kill/upload + diff (Round 4) Derived from live probing of the sanctioned oracle ([R40]–[R4B], ports 3140-3143, Tutorial/NSLPK3/KCL diff theory) plus a corpus key census. This section extends the dispatcher from the interactive read/proof handlers (§13) to the **entire** request path. Reproduced in `src/dispatch.rs` over the same `ProverOps` boundary (grown minimally) with one global version-index namespace. ### 14.1 Top-level (non-`/thy`) routes `src/route.rs::Toplevel` splits the surface into `/` · `/robots.txt` · `/favicon.ico` · `/kill` · `/static/**` · `/thy/…` · other. | route | method | response | |-------|--------|----------| | `GET /` | GET | `200` `text/html` index page (§7); no flash paragraph | | `POST /` | POST | `200` `text/html` index page; success flash `

Loaded new theory!

`, failure `…Post request failed.

` | | `GET /robots.txt` | GET | `200` `text/plain` body `User-agent: *` (13 bytes, no NL) | | `GET /favicon.ico` | GET | `303` → `/static/img/favicon.ico`, no-cache (see 14.4) | | `GET /kill?path=…` | GET | `200` `text/plain` `Canceled request!` (17 bytes, no NL); cancels a running search, server stays up | | `GET /kill` (no `path`) | GET | `400` `text/html` Invalid-Arguments page, body `
  • No path to kill specified!
    • \n
\n` | | `GET /static/` | GET | `200`, content-type by extension (14.5), body = file bytes; missing → `404` `text/plain` `File not found` | Wrong method on any of the above → `405` `text/html` **Method Not Supported** page (`

Method M not supported

`). These `405`/`400` pages and the `404` page share the standard head + tail (`src/errors.rs`, from `shell_template::SIMPLE_*`) and differ only in ``/`<h1>`/body — the same Yesod default-layout error family. ### 14.2 Theory-scoped additions | handler | method | response | |---------|--------|----------| | `download/{file}` | GET | `200` `application/octet-stream`, **no** `Content-Disposition`; body == the `source` body verbatim (the `{file}` segment is decorative) | | `reload` | POST | `200` JSON `{"redirect":"/thy/<kind>/<idx>/overview/help"}`; re-reads the theory **in place** at the same index | | `get_and_append/{file}` | POST | `200` JSON `{"alert":"Appended lemmas to <path>"}` (the third envelope shape; path non-deterministic) | | `edit/{verb}/{name}` | POST | structural edit (§13.3 / 14.6) | `source` and `message` are GET-only; a POST to them (or to `download`) is `405`. ### 14.3 Version lifecycle — one global index namespace (spec item: state) Every theory-version lives at a distinct index in **one** monotonically growing namespace (`= max ever allocated + 1`), regardless of how it was produced: * **Proof ops** (`method`, `diffMethod`, `autoprove*`) — new index, base retained. * **Upload** (`POST /`) — new index off the **same** counter (NSLPK3 → 5 when 1..4 existed). The response is the index page (200), **not** a redirect. * **Structural edits** and **`reload`** — mutate in place at the same index; the counter is untouched and other versions stay resolvable (a proof op after a reload of v1 still got index 3 when 1,2 existed). Reload does **not** reset the counter or drop modified versions. * Every allocated index stays resolvable forever (index-page window is a display cap only). ### 14.4 Redirect caching headers All `303 See Other` responses — `edit`/`add`/`delete` success, delete-not-found, and the favicon redirect — carry `Cache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate` and `Expires: Thu, 01 Jan 1970 05:05:05 GMT` (modelled by `Response.no_cache`). The JSON `{redirect}`/`{alert}` bodies (`method`, `autoprove`, `reload`, `get_and_append`) are ordinary `200`s with no cache headers. ### 14.5 Static content types `/static/**` is a filesystem handler (chunked, **no** caching headers). Content type is chosen by the last path segment's extension, **without** a `charset`: `.css`→`text/css`, `.js`→`application/javascript`, `.png`→`image/png`, `.ico`→`image/vnd.microsoft.icon`, no/unknown extension → `application/octet-stream` (e.g. `/static/LICENSE`). A missing file yields the plain-text `File not found` (not the dynamic HTML 404). `src/assets.rs::static_content_type`. ### 14.6 Structural-edit branches (refines §13.3) * **delete** `POST edit/delete/{name}`: lemma **found** → `303` → `overview/help` (removed in place); **not found** → `303` → `overview/delete/{name}` (theory unchanged). `delete_lemma` therefore returns `Option` (`None` = not found). * **edit** `POST edit/edit/{name}`: success → `303` → `overview/edit/{name}`; parse failure **or unknown lemma** → `200` full-page edit form (theory unchanged). * **add** `POST edit/add/{pos}`: success → `303` → `overview/add/{pos}`; failure → `200` add-form page. * **method/diffMethod** failure: `200` JSON `{"alert":"Sorry, but the prover failed on the selected method!"}`, **no** version bump (`apply_method` / `apply_diff_method` return `Option`). ### 14.7 Diff (observational-equivalence) mode — theory-kind `equiv` Started with `--diff`; the index links use `/thy/equiv/…`. The overview shell is the trace shell parameterised by `page::ShellKind::Equiv`: `<title>DiffTheory: NAME`, `/thy/equiv/` links, and **no** Actions "Append modified lemmas" item (MID/TAIL byte-identical to trace; reproduced byte-for-byte, `equiv_overview_shell_byte_identical`). New handlers: * `main/diffProof/{lemma}[/path…]` — JSON content pane (diff proof view). * `main/diffMethod/{lemma}/{n}[/path…]` — proof op → `200` JSON `{"redirect":"/thy/equiv/<new>/overview/diffProof/{lemma}/{focus}"}` (new version) or the `{alert}` on failure. * `main/diffrules` — JSON content pane. * `autoproveDiff/{strategy}/{bound}/diffProof/{lemma}[/side…]` — **no** all-sol flag (unlike trace `autoprove`). * `autoproveAll/{strategy}/{bound}` — autoprove every lemma. `intdot`/`interactive-graph-def` tails in diff mode carry a `graph/` prefix (opaque passthrough). autoproveDiff/autoproveAll **block the prover** on the probe theory, so their exact redirect bytes are unobservable here; they are dispatched as proof ops (new version) with the redirect modelled by analogy to `diffMethod`/`autoprove` (documented gap). ### 14.8 `del/path` and `verify` — the round-4 "absent" result was a PROBE-SHAPE ARTIFACT (resolved in Round 5) The round-4 sweep concluded `del`/`verify`/`path` return `404` for every method and shape. That was **wrong**: it used bogus segments (`x`/`y`/`z`) that never parse as theory paths, so every probe hit the "unparseable → route miss → `404`" branch. Both routes ARE registered in 1.13.0 and take a further **theory sub-path**: `del/path/<theory-path>` and `verify/<theory-path>`. A **parseable** theory-path with a wrong method answers `405` (not `404`) — proving registration ([R55]). They appear in zero manifest keys only because the crawler never followed these context-menu-triggered UI actions, not because they are unregistered. Full behaviour is pinned down in **§15**; `route.rs` now routes them to `Handler::DelPath` / `Handler::Verify` (the old `Handler::Other` → `404` fallback is retained only for genuinely-unparseable tails and non-`del/path`

// Own-authored observation-input theory (sealed room, this session).
// Designed to force never-captured proof-tree shapes through autoprove:
// multi-case branching (Fork1/Fork2), deeper chains (Hop), an attack tree
// (false_secret; ~x is published), and bounded/partial proofs.
theory TreeProbe
begin

builtins: hashing

rule Start:
  [ Fr(~x) ] --[ Begin(~x) ]-> [ St(~x), Out(~x) ]

rule Fork1:
  [ St(x) ] --[ Mid(x) ]-> [ Hop(x) ]

rule Fork2:
  [ St(x) ] --[ Mid(x) ]-> [ Hop(x) ]

rule HopOn:
  [ Hop(x) ] --[ Step(x) ]-> [ Fin(x) ]

rule End:
  [ Fin(x) ] --[ Done(x) ]-> []

lemma reach:
  exists-trace "Ex x #i. Done(x) @ #i"

lemma origin:
  all-traces "All x #i. Done(x) @ #i ==> Ex #j. Begin(x) @ #j"

lemma false_secret:
  all-traces "All x #i. Begin(x) @ #i ==> not(Ex #k. K(x) @ #k)"

end
